cross_connections.display_label: Both New Haven founders; both rejected the Half-Way Covenant.
Davenport envisioned Yale; Pierpont founded it — and married his granddaughter.
his granddaughter; first wife of Yale's founder

narrative_blocks.header: Fled England — founded New Haven Colony, 1638
narrative_blocks.body: Left England 1633, Holland, then Boston 1637. Co-founded New Haven Colony with Theophilus Eaton in 1638. Pastor First Church New Haven for thirty years.

narrative_blocks.header: Envisioned Yale — realized thirty years posthumously
narrative_blocks.body: Yale University was envisioned by Davenport, though his proposal was not realized until thirty years after his death. Rev. James Pierpont Sr. — who married Davenport's granddaughter Abigail — secured the Yale charter in 1701. Davenport College at Yale is named in his honor.

narrative_blocks.header: He hid the regicides Goffe and Whalley
narrative_blocks.body: When the regicides William Goffe and Edward Whalley fled in 1660, Davenport sheltered them in his New Haven home for over a month. He preached a sermon urging his congregation to hide the fugitives.

artworks.artwork_blurb: Portrait ca.1670; oldest work in Yale Art Gallery.

notable.notable_blurb: Co-founder New Haven Colony; envisioned Yale University

cross_connections.display_label: was his father-in-law and founded Newark

narrative_blocks.header: Born in exile — granddaughter married Yale's founder
narrative_blocks.body: Born in The Hague in 1635 while his father Rev. John Davenport Sr. was in Holland before emigrating to New England. His daughter Abigail Davenport married Rev. James Pierpont Sr. in 1691 — the man who secured the Yale charter in 1701, realizing his grandfather's vision.

parents.father_research_notes: Co-founded Southampton, Branford CT, and Newark NJ.

narrative_blocks.header: Her brother was Yale's first rector
narrative_blocks.body: Her father was Rev. Abraham Pierson Elder, who co-founded Southampton, Branford, and Newark. Her brother Abraham Pierson Jr. became the first rector of Yale College (1701-1707), teaching its first students in his home. She herself married John Davenport Jr., whose father Rev. John Davenport Sr. had envisioned a New Haven college thirty years before Yale was founded.

parents.father_research_notes: Son of Rev. John Davenport Sr. (X02123), co-founder of New Haven.

narrative_blocks.header: Granddaughter of the man who founded New Haven
narrative_blocks.body: Abigail Davenport was a granddaughter of Rev. John Davenport, who founded the New Haven Colony in 1638. In 1691 she became the first wife of Rev. James Pierpont.

narrative_blocks.header: She died of a cold months after the wedding
narrative_blocks.body: Abigail died in February 1692, at nineteen, the year after her marriage. She was the first of James Pierpont's three wives.

cross_connections.display_label: her grandfather; founder of New Haven Colony

burialCemetery.name: King's Chapel Burying Ground
burialCemetery.notes: Boston's oldest burying ground. Resting place of Rev. John Davenport (X02123), founder of the New Haven Colony.
